title: End-to-end smoke: Claude Code reads the brain without naming the source
userStory: As a user, I want three questions answered from the graph in Claude Code so that Phase 1 is provably end-to-end working.
acceptanceCriteria: In a client repo configured per US-012/US-013, ask three questions whose answers depend on migrated content (one factual lookup, one relationship traversal, one document-shaped retrieval)
Each question returns a relevant answer without explicitly naming Notion or any tool by name
.claude/logs/inject_memory.log shows the hook fired and returned hits for each prompt
Failure mode confirmed: turn the brain off and re-ask one question; the hook degrades silently and the prompt still executes
